description:
Index NOTES type names; it never resolves them. Runs over the in-house type-expr from .11. Two halves, one index-time activity, one commit.

DEFINE -- when building a scope (define_scope), reserve each @type in the FIRST walk: mint TypeName(module,name,arity), bind NamespaceSymbol::Type(TypeName), and hold its body type-expr together with the namespace captured at that point. Reserving in the first walk means sibling/forward type references are in scope -- no fixpoint. Add NamespaceSymbol::Type(TypeName).

REFERENCE -- walk every type-position in the indexed surface (@spec args/returns, param annotations, extern bodies, @type bodies, x-is-T tests) over the in-house type-expr, and resolve each name against the namespace captured at that declaration point: a Type binding -> reference_type(module,name,arity) (mirroring reference_function), recorded as an upstream dep of the consumer it feeds (the function whose contract/body names it, or the @type being defined). That recorded reference set IS the consumer later wait-set. An unbound lowercase name is a free type var (not referenced). Nothing resolves to Ty here; no type-algebra.

Retain top-level @type the way modules already do: finish_code_index (source.rs:58) drops program.attrs while modules keep theirs (source.rs:492) -- make GLOBAL uniform, so a top-level @type reserves into the GLOBAL scope.

Verify: namespace_test extended for Type; a @type becomes a referenceable identity with its body + captured namespace held; a @spec naming Foo.t records Foo.t as a dep of that function with nothing resolved; a free type var in a spec is not referenced.

comments.text: When wiring this in-house parser, REMOVE the module-level #![allow(dead_code)] at the top of src/compiler2/type_expr.rs -- it marks the not-yet-consumed parser from .11 and must come off once the reference walk (.1) and resolver (.3) consume it.

title: Note type names: NamespaceSymbol::Type, reference_type, retain top-level @type
